summary:
The primary center of autonomic nervous system activity lies within the hypothalamus, particularly the paraventricular nucleus, which coordinates broad autonomic outputs through descending pathways affecting heart rate, blood pressure, temperature, and hormone release. This makes the hypothalamus distinct from more specialized brainstem regions focused only on local reflexive controls.

Based on this analysis, option \boxed{\text{C}}—Hypothalamus—is correctly identified as the answer.

Okay, let me now re-express just the summary part without any extra commentary or formatting instructions.

---

The main center of autonomic nervous system activity is the hypothalamus, specifically the paraventricular nucleus, because it has descending neurons that directly regulate vital functions including heart rate, blood pressure, body temperature, and endocrine output via extensive neural connections throughout the body. Unlike isolated brainstem nuclei with narrow roles (e.g., urinary bladder function), the hypothalamus serves as an integrated command center essential for overall homeostatic balance.
